title: Kangaroo Level 5-6 - Practice Set 13: Advanced Problems
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: What is the value of 205 - 78 + 43?
A square has a perimeter of 36 cm. What is the length of one side?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='20' y='20' width='60' height='60' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A square with sides marked equal.
questions.solution: A square has four equal sides, so each side is 36 / 4 = 9 cm.

questions.question_text: In the sequence 4, 9, 14, 19, 24, ..., what is the eighth term?
questions.solution: Each term increases by 5. The eighth term is 4 + 7 * 5 = 39.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is equal to 3 * 4 + 5 * 2?
A ribbon is 2 meters long. If 65 cm is cut off, how much ribbon remains?
How many triangles are shown in the figure, which consists of a larger triangle split by one line from a vertex to the opposite side?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='10,80 90,80 50,10' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='50' y1='10' x2='50' y2='80' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A triangle with a line from the top vertex down to the base.
questions.solution: There are two smaller triangles and the one large triangle, for a total of 3.

questions.question_text: Emma is older than Nora but younger than Paula. Liam is older than Paula. Who is the youngest?
questions.solution: Order from youngest to oldest: Nora < Emma < Paula < Liam. So Nora is the youngest.

questions.question_text: What is the smallest two-digit number that is divisible by both 3 and 4?
questions.solution: The number must be divisible by 12. The smallest two-digit multiple of 12 is 12.

questions.question_text: A basket holds 6 apples. How many apples are in 15 such baskets?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='20' cy='30' r='10' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='50' cy='30' r='10' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='80' cy='30' r='10' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Three baskets representing a group of identical baskets.
questions.solution: 6 * 15 = 90 apples.

questions.question_text: If today is Wednesday, what day of the week will it be 30 days from now?
questions.solution: 30 divided by 7 leaves a remainder of 2. Two days after Wednesday is Friday.

questions.question_text: If 3x + 7 = 25, what is the value of x?
A rectangle has a length that is twice its width. If the perimeter is 48 cm, what is the area?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='10' y='20' width='100' height='50' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A rectangle longer horizontally than vertically.
questions.solution: If width = w, length = 2w. Perimeter = 2(w + 2w) = 6w = 48, so w = 8. Area = 8 * 16 = 128 cm^2.

questions.question_text: How many positive integers less than 50 are divisible by 6 but not by 4?
questions.solution: Multiples of 6 below 50: 6, 12, 18, 24, 30, 36, 42, 48. Those also divisible by 4 (i.e., by 12): 12, 24, 36, 48. That leaves 6, 18, 30, 42, which is 4 numbers.

questions.question_text: In how many different ways can the letters of the word KITE be arranged?
questions.solution: All 4 letters are distinct, so 4! = 24 arrangements.

questions.question_text: The ratio of red to blue marbles in a jar is 3 : 5. If there are 40 marbles in total, how many are red?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 40' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='15' cy='20' r='6' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='35' cy='20' r='6' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='55' cy='20' r='6' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='75' cy='20' r='6' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Four circles representing a portion of the marbles.
questions.solution: Total parts = 3 + 5 = 8. Red marbles = (3/8) * 40 = 15.

questions.question_text: Five friends finished a race. Tom finished before Alex. Sam finished after Ben. Alex finished before Ben. Kai finished last. Who finished first?
questions.solution: Tom < Alex < Ben < Sam, and Kai is last. So the order is Tom, Alex, Ben, Sam, Kai. Tom is first.

questions.question_text: A square is divided into 16 equal smaller squares. How many of those small squares touch the border of the large square?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='10' y='10' width='80' height='80' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='30' y1='10' x2='30' y2='90' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='50' y1='10' x2='50' y2='90' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='70' y1='10' x2='70' y2='90' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='10' y1='30' x2='90' y2='30' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='10' y1='50' x2='90' y2='50' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='10' y1='70' x2='90' y2='70' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A 4-by-4 grid of small squares inside a large square.
questions.solution: In a 4x4 grid, the inner squares form a 2x2 block of 4 squares. The border squares number 16 - 4 = 12.

questions.question_text: A shop sells pens in packs of 8 for $6. Mia needs 40 pens. How much does she pay?
The first three figures in a pattern use 4, 7, and 10 matchsticks. If the pattern continues in the same way, how many matchsticks does the seventh figure use?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 40' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='5' y='10' width='20' height='20' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='40' y='10' width='20' height='20' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='60' y='10' width='20' height='20' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='90' y='10' width='20' height='20' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='110' y='10' width='20' height='20' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Growing chain of squares made from matchsticks.
questions.solution: Each step adds 3 matchsticks. The nth term is 4 + 3(n - 1) = 3n + 1. For n = 7: 3 * 7 + 1 = 22.

questions.question_text: A movie started at 10:45 AM and lasted 1 hour and 50 minutes. At what time did it end?
What is the sum of all two-digit numbers that read the same forwards and backwards (palindromes)?
A rectangle is 12 cm by 8 cm. A smaller rectangle of 5 cm by 3 cm is cut out of one corner. What is the area of the remaining shape?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 130 90' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><path d='M 10 10 L 130 10 L 130 70 L 80 70 L 80 90 L 10 90 Z' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/></svg>
questions.image_description: An L-shaped region formed by removing a small rectangle from the corner of a larger rectangle.
questions.solution: Total area: 12 * 8 = 96. Cut out: 5 * 3 = 15. Remaining: 96 - 15 = 81 cm^2.

questions.question_text: The sum of three consecutive integers is 72. What is the largest of these integers?
questions.solution: Let the middle integer be n. Then (n-1) + n + (n+1) = 3n = 72, so n = 24. The largest is 25.

questions.question_text: How many different three-digit numbers can be formed using each of the digits 1, 2, 3, and 4 at most once?
questions.solution: Choose and arrange 3 digits from 4: 4 * 3 * 2 = 24 numbers.

questions.question_text: On an island, knights always tell the truth and knaves always lie. Three islanders, X, Y, and Z, make statements. X says, "Y is a knave." Y says, "Z is a knave." Z says, "X and Y are both knaves." How many of them are knights?
questions.solution: If X is a knight, Y is a knave, so Y lies; thus Z is a knight. But Z says X and Y are both knaves, which is false if X is a knight. Contradiction. So X is a knave, meaning Y is a knight. Then Z is a knave (from Y). Z lies, consistent with Z being a knave. Only Y is a knight, so 1 knight.

questions.question_text: A cube has edges of length 4 cm. It is painted on all sides and then cut into 1 cm cubes. How many small cubes have exactly two painted faces?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='20,30 60,30 60,80 20,80' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><polygon points='20,30 40,10 80,10 60,30' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><polygon points='60,30 80,10 80,60 60,80' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/></svg>
questions.solution: Small cubes with exactly two painted faces lie on edges but not at corners. Each edge has (4 - 2) = 2 such cubes, and a cube has 12 edges: 12 * 2 = 24.

questions.question_text: A tank fills at 12 liters per minute. A drain empties it at 4 liters per minute. If both run at the same time starting from empty, how many minutes to reach 120 liters?
questions.solution: Net fill rate = 12 - 4 = 8 liters per minute. Time = 120 / 8 = 15 minutes.

questions.question_text: In a class, the ratio of boys to girls is 4 : 5. After 6 boys join, the ratio becomes 1 : 1. How many girls are in the class?
questions.solution: Let boys = 4k, girls = 5k. After 6 boys join: 4k + 6 = 5k, so k = 6. Girls = 5 * 6 = 30.

questions.question_text: A row of 20 lamps is numbered 1 to 20 and all are initially off. First, every lamp is toggled. Then every second lamp is toggled. Then every third lamp is toggled. After these three rounds, how many lamps are on?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 30' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='15' cy='15' r='6' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='35' cy='15' r='6' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='55' cy='15' r='6' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='75' cy='15' r='6' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='95' cy='15' r='6' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='115' cy='15' r='6' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='135' cy='15' r='6' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='155' cy='15' r='6' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='175' cy='15' r='6' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/></svg>
questions.solution: A lamp is on if it is toggled an odd number of times. Lamp n is toggled in round 1 always, in round 2 if n is even, and in round 3 if n is a multiple of 3. Lamps on are those toggled an odd number of times: n that are odd and not multiples of 3, or n that are multiples of 6. Odd non-multiples of 3 up to 20: 1, 5, 7, 11, 13, 17, 19 (7 numbers). Multiples of 6 up to 20: 6, 12, 18 (3 numbers). Total on: 7 + 3 = 10.

questions.question_text: What is the units digit of 7^2026?
questions.solution: Units digits of 7^n cycle: 7, 9, 3, 1, 7, 9, 3, 1, ... with period 4. 2026 mod 4 = 2, so the units digit is the second in the cycle: 9.
